slides.title: Django Fundamentals
slides.subtitle:
The Web Framework for Perfectionists with Deadlines
Architecture, ORM, and Production Patterns

slides.name: Aravind Govindhasamy
slides.title: Software Developer
slides.experience: Specialist in Python, .NET, and Scalable Backend Systems
slides.oss_experience: Open Source Contributor & Technical Content Creator

list.text: Batteries Included: Auth, Admin, ORM, and Migrations are built-in.
Ridiculously Fast: Go from concept to launch in hours.
Reassuringly Secure: Helps developers avoid common security mistakes (SQLi, XSS, CSRF).

box.title: Born in a newsroom (Lawrence Journal-World) in 2003, Django was designed to handle fast-moving editorial content with robust technical foundations.

list.text: Explicit over Implicit: No magical auto-discovery that breaks.
Loose Coupling: Components are independent where possible.
Stability: Highly disciplined release cycle and LTS versions.

box.title: Every distinct piece of knowledge must have a single, unambiguous, authoritative representation within a system.

list.text: Model: The data access layer (ORM). Defines your data structure.
Template: The presentation layer. How the data looks (HTML/Django Template Language).
View: The bridge between Model and Template. Contains the business logic.

box.title: While similar to MVC (Model-View-Controller), Django uses a slightly different terminology that better reflects the role of the framework.

1. URL Dispatcher: Matches the URL to a View.
2. Middleware: Optional pre-processing of request.
3. View: Fetches data from Model.
4. Template: Renders the data.
5. Response: Sent back through middleware to the client.

list.text: Uniformity: Every Django project feels familiar.
Security Patches: Centralized updates for auth and security layers.
Compatibility: Integrated components are guaranteed to work together.

box.title: Why 'Batteries Included' matters in a microservices world.

list.text: Registry Pattern: Explicitly register apps and models.
Readable Settings: No hidden environment magic without your consent.

box.title: Django avoids 'magic' in favor of explicit configuration. This makes debugging and scaling significantly easier.

box.code:
from django.db import models

class Post(models.Model):
    title = models.CharField(max_length=200)
    slug = models.SlugField(unique=True)
    content = models.TextField()
    published = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    author = models.ForeignKey('User', on_delete=models.CASCADE)
box.title: In Django, your database schema is defined as Python classes. No SQL required (mostly).
Use the Meta class to define database-specific behavior and ordering.

list.text: makemigrations: Scans models and creates a new migration file.
migrate: Applies changes to the database.
showmigrations: List all migrations and their status.

box.title: Django Migrations track changes to your models and propagate them to your database schema.
Personalize how records are fetched by overriding get_queryset or adding custom methods.

list.text: select_related: Join-based optimization for ForeinKey.
prefetch_related: Separate query optimization for ManyToMany.
values_list('id', flat=True): Fetch only primary keys for massive memory savings.

box.code:
# Efficient fetching
posts = Post.objects.select_related('author').filter(active=True)
box.title: QuerySets are lazy and cached. Efficiency is key to prevent N+1 problems.

box.code:
from django.urls import path
from . import views

urlpatterns = [
    path('post/<slug:slug>/', views.post_detail, name='post-detail'),
]
box.title: Decouple your physical file structure from your URL structure.

list.text: Generic Views: ListView, DetailView, CreateView, etc.
Mixins: Add reusable features (LoginRequiredMixin).
Method Mapping: Automatically maps HTTP verbs (get, post) to methods.

box.title: While function-based views (FBVs) are simple, CBVs allow for reusable logic through inheritance and mixins.

box.code:
# Mixin usage
from django.contrib.auth.mixins import LoginRequiredMixin

class MyListView(LoginRequiredMixin, ListView):
    model = MyModel
box.title: Mixins provide a way to add extra functionality to class-based views, while decorators are typically used for function-based views.

box.code:
def get_context_data(self, **kwargs):
    context = super().get_context_data(**kwargs)
    context['now'] = timezone.now()
    return context
box.title: Override get_context_data in your CBV to add extra information to your template context.
Create your own mixins to encapsulate common cross-cutting concerns like logging or data filtering.

box.code:
from django import forms

class ContactForm(forms.Form):
    subject = forms.CharField(max_length=100)
    message = forms.CharField(widget=forms.Textarea)
    sender = forms.EmailField()
box.title: Django's form system handles data validation, security, and rendering with minimal code.
Custom validation logic lives in clean() or clean_() methods.
ModelForms automatically generate form fields from your Model definition, keeping your code DRY.

list.text: CSRF Protection: Token-based defense against cross-site request forgery.
SQL Injection: The ORM uses parameterized queries automatically.
XSS Protection: Templates escape HTML by default.

box.title: Django protects you from the most common web vulnerabilities out of the box.

list.text: Sessions: Server-side state. Great for standard web apps.
JWT: Stateless and portable. Best for APIs.

box.title: Django uses sessions by default, but for modern SPAs and Mobile apps, JWT (JSON Web Tokens) are often preferred.

list.text: HTTPS Only: Set SECURE_SSL_REDIRECT=True.
Secure Cookies: SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE=True.
HSTS: Enable HTTP Strict Transport Security.

box.title: Before going live, verify these essential security settings.

box.code:
from django.contrib.auth.models import AbstractUser

class User(AbstractUser):
    bio = models.TextField(blank=True)
    is_verified = models.BooleanField(default=False)
box.title: Always start your project with a custom user model. It's significantly harder to switch later.
Django comes with a full-featured authentication system: Users, Groups, Permissions, and a pluggable backend.

box.code:
def simple_middleware(get_response):
    def middleware(request):
        # Pre-processing
        response = get_response(request)
        # Post-processing
        return response
    return middleware
box.title: Middleware is a framework of hooks into Django’s request/response processing. It’s a light, low-level 'plugin' system.
Signals allow certain senders to notify a set of receivers that some action has taken place. Perfect for decoupling.

list.text: Blocking: Signals run in the same thread as the request.
Asynchronous: Use signals to trigger Celery tasks instead of doing work inside them.

box.title: Signals are synchronous by default. For heavy tasks, always offload to a task queue like Celery.

list.text: Per-site Caching: Cache the entire site.
Per-view Caching: Cache individual responses.
Template Fragment Caching: Cache specific parts of a page.

box.title: Django provides several levels of caching to boost performance in high-traffic applications.

box.code: CACHES = {"default": {"BACKEND": "django.core.cache.backends.redis.RedisCache"}}
box.title: Easily switch between different caching engines without changing your code.
Add your own custom scripts to manage.py for automation and scheduled tasks.
Serializers allow complex data such as QuerySets and model instances to be converted to native Python datatypes that can then be easily rendered into JSON/XML.

box.code:
from rest_framework.permissions import IsAuthenticated

permission_classes = [IsAuthenticated]
box.title: DRF provides flexible authentication and permission schemes to secure your API endpoints.

box.code:
from rest_framework import viewsets

class PostViewSet(viewsets.ModelViewSet):
    queryset = Post.objects.all()
    serializer_class = PostSerializer
box.title: Combine the logic for a set of related views in a single class. Routers automatically handle the URL conf.

list.text: WSGI (Gunicorn): Successful for years. Best for standard sync apps.
ASGI (Daphne/Uvicorn): Support for WebSockets, HTTP2, and async code.

box.title: Django supports both traditional synchronous and modern asynchronous server interfaces.

list.text: SECRET_KEY: Keep it secret, keep it safe.
ALLOWED_HOSTS: Prevent host header attacks.
Static Files: Use WhiteNoise or S3/CDN for assets.

box.title: Never deploy with DEBUG=True. Use environment variables for secrets.
Django's test runner is built on Python's unittest module, providing a test client to simulate requests.

list.text: Use Fat Models, Thin Views: Keep logic in models.
Keep Settings Modular: Separate base.py, dev.py, and prod.py.
Stay Updated: Django has a predictable security/LTS roadmap.

slides.title: Questions? 💬
slides.content:
Let's discuss Django architecture, the ORM, or production deployment!
Any Django-related queries welcome!

box.content:
🐍 Use Django to build something great.
Happy Coding!
